topic: How one founder recovered eight hours every week by automating business operations
format: Tell the story from the employee's perspective — what they experienced before and what changed for them after
post:
Every Sunday night, Layla dreaded Monday.

Not because of her workload. Because of the spreadsheets.

She was the only HR person at a 40-person company in Muscat. Every week: manual attendance checks, salary calculations, WPS file building, back-and-forth with finance. Four hours gone before she could touch anything strategic.

Then the founder switched to SmartPro.

Week one, Layla ran payroll in 23 minutes. WPS submitted directly to the bank. Ministry of Manpower records updated automatically. No spreadsheet. No back-and-forth.

The founder got his time back too — 8 hours a month he'd been spending reviewing, approving, and chasing errors that shouldn't have existed.

He told me: "I didn't realise how much of my week was just fixing admin."

That's the real cost nobody talks about. It's not the software subscription. It's the 8 hours every week your best people spend doing work a system should be doing.

If your HR team is still building WPS files by hand, the problem isn't effort. It's the tool.

What's one task in your operations that you keep tolerating — even though you know there's a better way?
